name: Customer.io API
what_it_does: Behavioral messaging platform for sending automated email, SMS, push notifications, and in-app messages triggered by user events, with a Track API for event ingestion and Transactional API for on-demand sends.
use_cases: Triggering event-based email/SMS from agent-detected user actions
Sending transactional messages with dynamic content via API
Managing customer data and attributes programmatically
Creating and updating segments based on behavioral data
A/B testing message variants via API
not_for: High-volume bulk marketing without behavioral triggers
Teams needing deep ecommerce catalog integration (use Klaviyo)
Simple transactional-only email without automation needs
best_when: You need event-driven, multi-channel messaging (email + SMS + push) with sophisticated behavioral segmentation triggered by agent actions.
avoid_when: You only need simple transactional email or are in ecommerce and need catalog-level personalization.

auth.notes: Track API uses site ID + API key (Basic auth). Transactional and App APIs use Bearer token with API key. Two different auth patterns across API surfaces.

pricing.notes: No free tier. Essentials plan starts at $100/month for up to 5k profiles. Pricing scales with profile count. Trial available.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clear API errors with meta and errors arrays. Documentation includes error code reference.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Event tracking uses unique event IDs for deduplication. Customer creation is idempotent by ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Two different auth methods for Track API vs Transactional API - easy to mix up
Customer IDs are immutable once set - agent must plan identity strategy upfront
Liquid templating in messages requires understanding the template language
No free tier - requires paid plan from day one
Profile limit on lower tiers can be hit by agent-created test customers

security_score_components.security_notes: App API key + site ID. Tracking API key for event collection. SOC2 Type II. Customer behavioral data — PII concerns. GDPR/CCPA deletion APIs. Separate keys for tracking vs management.
